concept: Allocate / Assign Resources, Access, Time, or Burdens
description: To assign resources, access, time, or burdens under a stated criterion. The word stays neutral on purpose: an allocation can be unequal, coerced, wasteful, or perfectly fair by one standard and rejected by another.
sound_symbolism: Bilabial breath opens 'pha' into broad 'a'. Stressed 'nu' gathers that openness at the teeth and gives it depth, a contour close enough to 'phanoi' (portion) for the ear to hear the family. Final 'li' releases the tongue into a light bright line. One field of breath becomes a held share and then moves toward its place.
grammatical_notes: A transitive verb with the allocating agent as subject and the assigned resource or burden as object: 'ha kowela napuro phanuli.' (The council allocates grain.) State recipients, uses, amounts, timing, criterion, rejected claims, and source separately. The event-noun rule supplies allocation. Distinct from 'phanoi' (portion), 'phowe' (share), delivery, 'shereni' (entitlement), and fair distribution.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Commons governance requires explicit allocation of scarce and abundant resources across present needs, future regeneration, maintenance, and unequal burdens.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: A value-neutral allocation term keeps the criterion and rejected claims visible instead of allowing words for sharing or fairness to endorse the outcome in advance.

semantic_domains.activity: assigning a resource, opportunity, or burden under a criterion
semantic_domains.community: collective allocation among stated recipients or purposes
